nationalEstimates.offense: Offenses against the family and children

tenYearTrends.offense: Offenses against the family and children

bySex.offense: Offenses against the family and children

byRace.offense: Offenses against the family and children

byEthnicity.offense: Offenses against the family and children
1Because of rounding, the percentages may not add to 100.0.
2The ethnicity totals are representative of those agencies that provided ethnicity breakdowns. Not all agencies provide ethnicity data; therefore, the race and ethnicity totals will not equal.
3Violent crimes are offenses of murder and nonnegligent manslaughter, rape, robbery, and aggravated assault. Property crimes are offenses of burglary, larceny-theft, motor vehicle theft, and arson.

4Drunkenness figures were only submitted by agencies via the Summary Reporting System (SRS). As of 2021, drunkenness is no longer a separate offense in the National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS), but it is included with All Other Offenses (except traffic) category.
5Figures for the category of suspicion include only data submitted by agencies to the SRS, because suspicion data is not collected as a crime category via NIBRS.

3Violent crimes are offenses of murder and nonnegligent manslaughter, rape, robbery, and aggravated assault. Property crimes are offenses of burglary, larceny-theft, motor vehicle theft, and arson.

4Drunkenness figures were only submitted by agencies via the Summary Reporting System (SRS). As of 2021, drunkenness is no longer a separate offense in the National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS), but it is included with All Other Offenses (except traffic) category.
5Figures for the category of suspicion include only data submitted by agencies to the SRS, because suspicion data is not collected as a crime category via NIBRS.
